verdicts.evidence: server/ai-context-check.ts:29-46 (ContextCompleteness { score 0-100, connected, total, sources[] }), :80-123 includes a 'brand-voice' source scoring voice guidelines + brand-docs count
verdicts.correction: The narrow claim is correct — no BRAND-scoped completeness % exists (rg brandCompleteness / completenessScore-brand → 0 hits; server/intelligence/brand-slice.ts emits no completeness/score/percent). BUT the gatherer missed reusable prior art: server/ai-context-check.ts already implements a general workspace 'AI Context Completeness' engine (checkAIContext → 0-100 score with per-source status/detail/impacts, exposed via server/routes/ai.ts:112). It even scores brand-voice as one source. A brand cockpit % should EXTEND this pattern (or add brand-scoped sources to it), not build a fresh scorer from scratch. effort 'M' is fair, but the whereItLives ('brand slice or a projection on GET /api/workspaces/:id') is wrong — the natural home is ai-context-check.ts, and there is already a dedicated GET route for it.

verdicts.evidence: server/routes/brandscript.ts:161 POST :id/complete is the only AI section route; completeBrandscript (server/brandscript.ts:211-) takes only (workspaceId, brandscriptId), filters emptySections and returns early if none empty — no per-section/'redo a filled section' parameter. The prompt path is reusable for a per-section variant, matching the whereItLives note. effort 'S' holds (no schema/migration).

server/routes/brand-identity.ts:68 GET /:workspaceId/export reads ?tier and calls exportDeliverables (server/brand-identity.ts:382), which lists ALL approved deliverables (status==='approved') and joins to one markdown blob — no per-item route or type/id param. Route census (56/68/81/88/111/138) has no single-item export. effort 'S' holds.

rg -in 'interview' server shared → single hit at server/discovery-ingestion.ts:199 (a prompt string: 'focus on what the CLIENT said (not the interviewer)'). No interview/Q&A store, migration, or route. discovery-ingestion route census (server/routes/discovery-ingestion.ts:70-209) covers sources/text/process/extractions only — no interview endpoint. effort 'M' (new store + migration + ai-operation entry) holds.

The KNOWLEDGE_BASE_GENERATION job (shared/types/background-jobs.ts:16) is implemented in server/workspace-context-generation-job.ts:89 as `const { scraped } = await scrapeWorkspaceSite(ws)` then AI-summarizes SCRAPED WEBSITE content (:96-143) — it never reads discovery sources/extractions. Discovery extraction (server/discovery-ingestion.ts:176+) produces voice_pattern/story_element extractions, NOT a knowledge base. No source-fed KB path exists. effort 'M' holds.

brandscripts table (server/db/migrations/053-brandscript-engine.sql:14-21) has columns id/workspace_id/name/framework_type/created_at/updated_at — NO status column; rg 'ALTER TABLE brandscripts' → 0 migrations. server/brandscript.ts has no status/approved code. server/routes/brandscript.ts has no approve route. state-machines register brand_deliverable (BRAND_DELIVERABLE_TRANSITIONS, state-machines.ts:532/597 — a DIFFERENT entity: brand_identity_deliverables draft↔approved) but NO brandscript entity. effort 'S' UNDERSTATED: adding a status column triggers the DB-column+mapper lockstep rule (migration + rowToBrandscript mapper + write path + state-machine registration + transitions test) — realistically M.
verdicts.correction: Direction and 'does not exist today' are correct, but effort 'S' should be M: per CLAUDE.md's 'DB column + mapper lockstep' + 'status transitions must use state machines' rules, this needs migration SQL + row mapper update + write path + registerTransitionTable + a transitions guard test, not a trivial change. Also note brand_identity_deliverables ALREADY has a draft↔approved state machine (state-machines.ts:532) — the brandscript work should mirror that pattern rather than invent a new one.

verdicts.evidence: Server enforces .txt/.md by BOTH extension and mimetype: server/routes/discovery-ingestion.ts:93-97 (extOk = ext==='txt'||'md'; mimeOk = ACCEPTED_MIMETYPES.has(file.mimetype); rejects if either fails), and reads via fs.readFileSync(..., 'utf-8') (:112) — no PDF/DOCX/XLSX binary parser. Client mirrors this (src/components/brand/DiscoveryTab.tsx handleFiles: ext==='txt'||'md'). effort 'M' (upload path + parsing dependency) holds.
